---
id: update-automation
title: "Edit An Automation"
description: "Edit automation name, enabled status, and configuration."
sidebar_label: "Edit An Automation"
hide_title: true
hide_table_of_contents: true
api: 
sidebar_class_name: "patch api-method"
info_path: versioned_docs/version-Beta/aqara-developer/data-export-api/data-export-api
custom_edit_url: null
---

import MethodEndpoint from "@theme/ApiExplorer/MethodEndpoint";
import ParamsDetails from "@theme/ParamsDetails";
import RequestSchema from "@theme/RequestSchema";
import StatusCodes from "@theme/StatusCodes";
import OperationTabs from "@theme/OperationTabs";
import TabItem from "@theme/TabItem";
import Heading from "@theme/Heading";

<Heading
  as={"h1"}
  className={"openapi__heading"}
  children={"Edit An Automation"}
>
</Heading>

<MethodEndpoint
  method={"patch"}
  path={"/automations/{automationId}"}
  context={"endpoint"}
>
  
</MethodEndpoint>



Edit automation name, enabled status, and configuration.

:::tip
You can edit the name and enabled status of all automations, but you can only edit the configuration for SCRIPT_JSON automations.
:::


<Heading
  id={"request"}
  as={"h2"}
  className={"openapi-tabs__heading"}
  children={"Request"}
>
</Heading>

<ParamsDetails
  parameters={[{"in":"path","name":"automationId","description":"Automation ID.","required":true,"schema":{"type":"string"}}]}
>
  
</ParamsDetails>

<RequestSchema
  title={"Body"}
  body={{"required":true,"content":{"application/json":{"schema":{"type":"object","required":["updateMask"],"properties":{"name":{"type":"string","description":"New name.","nullable":true},"enabled":{"type":"boolean","description":"New enabled state. true: enabled; false: disabled.","nullable":true},"config":{"type":"object","description":"New configuration. Only applicable to automations with `definitionType` of `SCRIPT_JSON`.","nullable":true,"additionalProperties":true},"updateMask":{"type":"array","description":"List of parameter names to be updated. Please make sure each item in <code>updateMask</code> has a corresponding new value provided in the data object.<br />\n**Note**: `name` and `enabled` are applicable to all automations. `config` is only applicable to automations with `definitionType` of `SCRIPT_JSON`.\n","items":{"type":"string","enum":["name","enabled","config"]}}},"title":"UpdateAutomationRequest"}}}}}
>
  
</RequestSchema>

  id={undefined}
  label={undefined}
